I know I’m getting old. I find myself cranky about everything. Anyway, not that I want to go back to paper magazines, but they used to have profiles on producers in the publications. I can’t seem to find anything anymore in either Vinous or RP.

Isn’t cranky old man redundant?

William Kelley does producer profiles for Burgundy producers. If you want great and long producer profiles, read John Gilman’s A View From the Cellar.

posts.name: Chris_Rickey
posts.cooked:
What do you mean by producer profiles? Both Vinous and Wine Advocate include profiles of some producers in connection with reviews; not consistently, and some are more voluminous than others (e.g. David Schildknecht). In Wine Advocate, the producer profiles are found at the bottom of the reviews. In Vinous, in the “classic” view, you have to hover over the producer to see the producer profiles; in the new view, the profile is at the top of the page when you click on a producer and also at the bottom of each review.

If you mean long-form profiles of a producer, those were always pretty rare, but both publications do have them from time to time, mainly in the guise of verticals. Monica Larner does producer profiles frequently which are not verticals.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
I know I’m getting old. I find myself cranky about everything. Anyway, not that I want to go back to paper magazines, but they used to have profiles on producers in the publications. I can’t seem to find anything anymore in either Vinous or RP.

Got a vertical of Tremblay Chapelle back to 2005, and Lamy Haute Densité back to 06, coming out this month! Both using those cuvées as a window onto the producers’ evolution and way of working.

It certainly seems that way sometimes. But women are actually allowed, too.

Hi Sarah,

I certainly didn’t mean to downplay the role of woman on the board. And in general, people are warm, generous, and knowledgeable in their posts. But I do think there is a level of pettiness and looking for a fight that I wish would disappear from the board, as it is perpetuated by some cranky old men. That’s what I was trying to convey.

Cheers,

Hal

Very funny responses. Made me smile. But just for a minute ! Then I went back to yelling at clouds.

I agree Burgundy seems to be the exception. In fact I’d hold Burghound up to be the best example. He does a little interview with each producer about the vintage and any changes at the winery since lat year. Tanzer and WA use to do the same by my memory.
